Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 984150f3-3aab-4a25-b623-d6e7472b4dd3.

Resolves #10169

This PR implements autonomous extraction of `TAGGED_CONCEPT` edges for the A2A messaging infrastructure. Now, every message processed by `MailboxService` runs a non-blocking asynchronous semantic extraction routine, generating Native Edge Graph links directly connecting messages to architectural concepts without stalling delivery.

## Deltas from ticket (if any)
No major deltas. We extended `SemanticGraphExtractor` to extract concepts and integrated it directly into `MailboxService` as planned. The extraction uses the fallback logic gracefully emitting no concepts in case of LLM parsing failures.

## Test Evidence
`npx playwright test test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/MailboxService.spec.mjs`
Result: 10 passed
`npx playwright test test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/services/SemanticGraphExtractor.spec.mjs`
Result: 10 passed (including 4 new tests specifically verifying `extractMessageConcepts` and fallback flows).

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] Ensure cross-agent messaging successfully auto-emits concepts to the live SQLite db.

## Comments

> ❋ # PR Review Summary
> 
> **Status:** Request Changes
> 
> **Peer-Review Opening:** Nice extractor extension — the fire-and-forget design is the right call for not stalling delivery, and the retry-with-self-correction loop mirrors the existing Gemma4 pattern cleanly. The `auto_extracted: true` property + lower 0.8 weight for auto-emitted concepts is a thoughtful distinction from manually-curated edges. One blocker + a few non-blocking observations below.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
> 
> *   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 85 — Fire-and-forget non-blocking design is the correct paradigm for side-effect enrichment; `auto_extracted` flag on the node carries the provenance signal into the graph; lower edge weight differentiates auto-extraction from manually-curated links. 15 deducted because the `CONCEPT:` / `CLASS:` uppercase prefix convention isn't reconciled with the open node-ID case canonicalization debt on `#10172` — the PR commits new edges in the uppercase form while that scope is unsettled.
> *   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 78 — `extractMessageConcepts` JSDoc is functional (param + return documented) but single-line and lacks Anchor & Echo: no `@summary`, no `@see` cross-reference to the `MailboxService.addMessage` consumer that is the sole caller, no architectural context tying the method to the Concept Ontology substrate. 22 deducted for the missing cross-service anchor + the "Deltas from ticket" section understating the novel `auto_extracted` property contract.
> *   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 72 — Retry state machine is solid; input-guard (empty/non-string body → `[]`) is defensive; graceful API-offline fallback is tested. 28 deducted for two gaps: (a) no integration test covers the `MailboxService.addMessage` wire-up — the sole test surface is the extractor unit; (b) both new tests monkey-patch `OpenAiCompatible.prototype.generate` and restore in-band; a thrown expectation would leak the mutation into the next test in the serial run.
> *   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 90 — `#10169` AC fully satisfied: extraction wired into `addMessage`, fallback is graceful, tests assert both happy path and failure path. 10 deducted for the implicit cross-reference between extractor method and consumer — no @see link makes the connection discoverable.
> *   **`[IMPACT]`**: 75 — Auto-emission of TAGGED_CONCEPT edges feeds the Hybrid GraphRAG / DreamService / Librarian navigation layer from A2A conversation surface, which is meaningful downstream reach. Not architectural-pillar since it extends an established extractor substrate rather than introducing a new paradigm.
> *   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 55 — Medium: retry-with-self-correction state machine duplicates the pattern from the existing `extractConcepts` path; fire-and-forget promise boundary requires readers to internalize the "delivery succeeds, extraction eventually settles" semantics; auto-seed-then-link inside the `.then()` is a small but non-trivial graph-mutation sequence.
> *   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Quick Win — discrete enhancement on existing substrate, meaningful downstream unlock, minimal blast radius outside the single consumer wire-up.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
> 
> *   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves `#10169`
> *   **Related Graph Nodes:** Parent `#10143` (Graph-first Memory artifacts); adjacent `#10172` (node-ID case canonicalization — the CONCEPT:/CLASS: uppercase the PR commits is inside the canonicalization scope); sibling `#10165` (Gemma4 extractor provenance edges — established the retry pattern this PR reuses); `#10139` (Mailbox epic — consumer surface).
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> *   **`[KB_GAP]`**: The `auto_extracted: true` property introduced on auto-generated `CONCEPT:` / `CLASS:` nodes is a net-new graph signal with no reference in `learn/agentos/ConceptOntology.md` or the DreamService consumer code paths. Downstream consumers reading concept nodes have no way to distinguish auto from curated. Worth a follow-up docs note.
> *   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: None surfaced by the PR itself — the retry path is well-tested. Test-isolation pattern (prototype mutation without `try/finally`) is a reusable concern worth addressing repo-wide rather than in this PR.
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The retry-with-corrective-user-message pattern here is a good empirical template for other LLM-call sites. Duplicated locally instead of abstracted — an eventual refactor could DRY the two call sites, but not blocking.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge:** `MailboxService.addMessage` now fires `extractMessageConcepts(body)` without awaiting the result and without back-pressure on concurrent messages. A burst of A2A traffic (workflow-heavy session) queues N concurrent LLM calls against the provider. For a local MLX/Ollama endpoint, concurrent requests serialize at the server boundary with no visible queue; for the Gemini cloud provider on the free tier, concurrent calls easily trip the rate-limit. The PR has graceful-failure semantics per-call, but no coordination — every failed extraction is a silently-missing TAGGED_CONCEPT edge. Consider either (a) a bounded in-flight counter, or (b) handing off to an existing lazy-edge queue (`#10165`) where the extraction re-runs after the dust settles.
> 
> **Unverified assumption:** `c.split(':').slice(1).join(':')` correctly recovers the `name` for `CLASS:Neo.component.Base`-shaped IDs (three colons in the ID space is structurally possible for future `CLASS:some-ns:subns:Name` patterns). Current tests don't exercise a multi-colon CLASS ID — the regex-equivalent would be `c.replace(/^[^:]+:/, '')`. Low risk today, non-blocking, but brittle if the ontology ever namespaces further.
> 
> **Edge case:** Provider returns `{concepts: null}` explicitly (not just missing). `Array.isArray(null) === false` so the schema-validation branch catches it, retry fires, second attempt fails the same way, return `[]`. Safe, tested — noting because I looked.
> 
> **Follow-up concern:** The `CONCEPT:` / `CLASS:` uppercase convention here predates `#10172`'s case-canonicalization settlement. If `#10172` lands on lowercase canonical (Opus-leaning option A per the 2026-04-21 handover), this PR's auto-emitted edges become the wrong case and need migration. Not a blocker for this PR — but #10172 should reference this write-path when it reshapes.
> 
> ---
>

> - [x] If a new MCP tool is added, is it documented in the relevant skill's reference payload? — N/A (no new MCP tool)
> - [x] If a new convention is introduced, is the convention documented somewhere? — The `auto_extracted: true` property convention is undocumented outside this PR
> 
> **Findings:** Minor documentation gap surfaced in Required Actions; no cross-skill integration misses.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 📋 Required Actions
> 
> To proceed with merging, please address the following:
> 
> - [ ] **Integration test for the `MailboxService.addMessage` wire-up.** The sole test surface today is `SemanticGraphExtractor.spec.mjs`. Given that `#10174`'s SENT_TO persistence bug was precisely the "tested the unit, didn't test the wire-up" class of failure, a test that invokes `addMessage` and verifies `TAGGED_CONCEPT` edges materialize (with provider mocked) closes the same gap this time. The fire-and-forget promise makes the test slightly subtle — either `await` a small settling delay, or expose a `Promise` for test instrumentation.
> - [ ] **Test isolation: wrap prototype-mutation in `try/finally`** in both new `SemanticGraphExtractor.spec.mjs` tests. Current pattern restores inside the happy path only; a thrown `expect` would leak the mock into the next test in serial mode.
> 
> Non-blocking (happy to take these as follow-up tickets if preferred):
> 
> - Rate-limit / back-pressure coordination for concurrent `extractMessageConcepts` calls (see Depth Floor). Could be filed against `#10165`'s lazy-edge-queue scope.
> - Doc note in `learn/agentos/ConceptOntology.md` about the `auto_extracted: true` property — distinguishing auto vs curated at read time.
> - `#10172` coordination on CONCEPT:/CLASS: case — cross-reference this PR's write path when the canonical case settles.
> 
> Thanks for taking `#10169` — this lands the auto-enrichment substrate that lets DreamService mine A2A traffic for concept density without manual tagging discipline on the agent side.
>

> ### ✅ Required Actions Verification
> 
> **Required Action 1 — Integration test for `addMessage` wire-up:** `[ADDRESSED]`
> 
> The new test `addMessage auto-emits TAGGED_CONCEPT edges via SemanticGraphExtractor` in `MailboxService.spec.mjs` exercises the full wire-up:
> - Mocks `SemanticGraphExtractor.extractMessageConcepts` to return two deterministic concepts (one `CONCEPT:*`, one `CLASS:*`)
> - `await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 0))` after `addMessage` yields long enough for the fire-and-forget `.then()` to settle
> - Asserts both TAGGED_CONCEPT edges exist on the message node, and that the auto-seeded `CONCEPT:mcp-integration` node carries `properties.auto_extracted === true`
> - Restored via `try/finally` so a thrown expectation doesn't leak the mock
> 
> This is exactly the test-surface that was missing on `#10147` / `#10174` and which let the SENT_TO bug through for months. Shutting that class of failure down a second time — nice.
> 
> **Required Action 2 — Test isolation (try/finally for prototype mutation):** `[ADDRESSED]`
> 
> Both `SemanticGraphExtractor.spec.mjs` tests (`should extract concepts from message bodies`, `should handle API failure gracefully during message concept extraction`) now wrap the `OpenAiCompatible.prototype.generate` mutation in `try { mutate; assert; } finally { restore; }`. Restoration is now guaranteed regardless of assertion outcome.
> 
> ---
>

Worth noting as an empirical pattern for future async-wire-up testing.
